methods.description: Process for measuring physicochemical variables on site and collecting biological samples for DNA preservation and morphological identification.

steps.description: Several physicochemical variables were measured on site, including dissolved oxygen (DO), pH, conductivity, total dissolved solids (TDS), and temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: measure environmental variables using multiparameter device

steps.description: We collected nine Surber samples (0.11 m2 each, 1 m2 total per site) per site using a 280 microns mesh size.
steps.searchable_summary: collect Surber samples for biological analysis

steps.description: Samples were fixed in 96% alcohol for DNA preservation.
steps.searchable_summary: fix samples in alcohol for DNA preservation

steps.description: We identified Chironomids to genus-level and separated them into morphotypes.
steps.searchable_summary: identify and separate Chironomids into morphotypes

steps.description: Heads were slide-mounted using the Epler's method, and these preparations were used to confirm morphological identification using specialized bibliography.

methods.description: Analysis of environmental data to understand variability and its influence on Polypedilum assemblage structure.

steps.description: We used the measured physicochemical parameters in a Principal Component Analysis (PCA).
Conducted a correlation analysis between variables to find a correlation between elevation and temperature and between conductivity and TDS values.
steps.searchable_summary: perform correlation analysis on environmental variables

steps.description: Overlapped a cluster analysis with a cluster of Euclidean distances similarity to show site groupings based on environmental variability.
steps.searchable_summary: perform cluster analysis on environmental data

steps.description: Performed a Canonical Correspondence Analysis (CCA) to explore the influence of environmental characteristics on Polypedilum OTUs assemblages.
steps.searchable_summary: perform CCA on environmental and OTU data

methods.description: Methods for delimiting species and conducting phylogenetic analysis using genetic data.

steps.description: We obtained a 474-bp-long fragment from the mtCO1 gene from 68 Polypedilum sp. specimens in eight sampling stations.
steps.searchable_summary: extract mtCO1 gene fragment from specimens

steps.description: Sequence comparisons against databases indicated an identity match of 85% to 90% with previously entered Polypedilum sequences.
The ABGD method produced the same results for the JC69 and K2P models.
steps.searchable_summary: apply ABGD method for species delimitation

steps.description: The phylogenetic analysis supported the groups inferred from the ABGD method.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct phylogenetic analysis to support ABGD groups
